changeScope: Attempt 2 added only a bold title line '**Non-nullable AccessSettings**' to the note callout at line 51. No other content changed from attempt 1.

claims.claim: Title 'Non-nullable AccessSettings' accurately describes the note content
claims.location: Note callout in 'Assign handlers to events' section, line 51 (bold title)
claims.evidence: The AccessSettings property on all 14 event data state classes enforces non-null via ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(value) on the internal setter. 'Non-nullable' accurately describes the runtime behavior: the property cannot be assigned null. 'AccessSettings' is the exact property name (e.g., CreateContentItemEventDataState.cs line 51, PublishWebPageEventDataState.cs line 66). The title is a precise summary of the note body.

claims.claim: Content item and web page event data states expose an AccessSettings property
claims.evidence: All 8 content engine event data state classes (e.g., CreateContentItemEventDataState.cs line 51) and 6 web page event data state classes (e.g., PublishWebPageEventDataState.cs line 66) have a public AccessSettings property.

claims.claim: AccessSettings property is of type ContentAccessSettings
claims.evidence: All AccessSettings properties are typed as ContentAccessSettings. Confirmed in CreateContentItemEventDataState.cs line 51: 'public ContentAccessSettings AccessSettings', PublishWebPageEventDataState.cs line 66: same. ContentAccessSettings.cs defines the sealed class in CMS.ContentEngine namespace.

claims.claim: This property requires a non-null value – setting it to null throws an ArgumentNullException
claims.evidence: ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(value) present on internal setter in all 14 event data state classes. Content engine examples: CreateContentItemEventDataState.cs line 56, UpdateDraftEventDataState.cs line 50. Web page examples: PublishWebPageEventDataState.cs line 71, DeleteWebPageEventDataState.cs line 71, MoveWebPageEventDataState.cs line 83, UnpublishWebPageEventDataState.cs line 71.

claims.claim: Use ContentAccessSettings.Public() for publicly accessible content
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs line 31: 'public static ContentAccessSettings Public() => new(false);' with XML doc: 'Creates content access settings for public (unsecured) content. No authentication required.' All event data state backing fields default to ContentAccessSettings.Public() (e.g., CreateContentItemEventDataState.cs line 15, PublishWebPageEventDataState.cs line 18).

claims.claim: Cross-reference to 'Reference - Global system events' page for affected events and access modes
claims.evidence: The page link identifier 2AOiCQ is a documentation-internal reference. Consistent with pre-existing usage of the same identifier on line 45 of this page. Cannot verify target page content from source code.

inlineCodeElements.notes: Matches property name in all event data state classes.
Matches class name: ContentAccessSettings.cs in CMS.ContentEngine namespace.

Matches static factory method: ContentAccessSettings.cs line 31.

preExistingObservations.observation: Pre-existing code example handler type mismatch
preExistingObservations.details: Handler Page_Create_After uses CreateWebPageLanguageVariantEventArgs, but WebPageEvents.Create expects CreateWebPageEventArgs. Pre-existing issue NOT introduced by TASK-015.

regressionCheck.notes: Compared attempt 2 against attempt 1 approved content. The only change is the addition of bold title '**Non-nullable AccessSettings**' on line 51. All previously-verified note body content is unchanged. No accuracy regressions detected.

invariantSupremacy.notes: No conflicts between meta-knowledge/research and policy invariants.

summary.narrative: Attempt 2 accuracy review: APPROVED. The only change from attempt 1 was adding the bold title '**Non-nullable AccessSettings**' to the note callout. This title is technically accurate — 'Non-nullable' correctly describes the runtime enforcement (ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull on all 14 event data state setters), and 'AccessSettings' is the exact property name. All 5 verifiable claims in the note body remain unchanged and verified against source code at commit 65412d25. The 1 unverifiable claim is a cross-reference to an internal doc page. No accuracy regressions from attempt 1.
